sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 49g. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 49g. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 10.7g. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, you want real nutrient concentration, not just tiny-food hype.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 10.7g. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, you want real nutrient concentration, not just tiny-food hype.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 30.2g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 141% DV, zinc at 71% DV, iron at 49% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. calorie density still stacks up fast because tiny foods can still overshoot fast. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.

closing.summary: Pumpkin Seeds does have some solid upside, especially tiny food with serious nutrient density and easy to sprinkle into meals. but calorie density still stacks up fast and real serving sizes are often small keep it from climbing higher. best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.useCaseNote: Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
